subject: Ecology
title: Vernal Pools
description: A quick win: understand Vernal Pools.

hook.text: Vernal pools appear in spring, last a few months, and vanish by summer—but they're ecological gold.

buildup.text: Because they dry out, fish can't survive in them—making them safe nurseries for amphibians.

discovery.text: Spotted salamanders, fairy shrimp, and wood frogs depend entirely on these temporary pools.

twist.text: Many vernal pool species exist nowhere else—they evolved specifically for this fleeting habitat.

climax.text: Development that fills or paves vernal pools can eliminate species with no backup habitat.

punchline.text: The most temporary habitat holds the most irreplaceable life.

quiz.question: Why are vernal pools safe for amphibian larvae?
options: Fish can't survive because pools dry out
The water is too hot for predators
